mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical 2.0% annualized trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive skew when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals aggressive monetary tightening, which immediately raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows no significant negative return differential when FEDFUNDS jumps > 0.25pp month-over-month over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration, which triggers corporate earnings revisions downward and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which boost corporate margins and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns do not materialize when INDPRO expands during identified post-shock recovery periods, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real personal savings and financial assets reflects household balance sheet strength, which supports consumer spending and equity demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates across any 2-year rolling estimation window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.10 million annualized units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which drags down broader economic activity and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops below 1100, or the threshold signal produces a negative information coefficient over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent spikes in CPI and the federal funds rate indicate stagflationary pressure, which simultaneously erodes real corporate cash flows and elevates discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint CPI-FEDFUNDS signal fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns when both series rise simultaneously, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during an early recession phase triggers risk-off positioning and flight-to-safety flows, which immediately depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets do not decline over the subsequent week when UNRATE rises during identified early recession periods, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a 3-year walk-forward test.
